CEO will endeavor to implement the audit recommendation for CY 2022 procurement projects.
observations.reason: Committed to implement for CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the OIC – City Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for the account Due from NGAs-PhilHealth Claims and regularly update the same and ensure that all claims from PhilHealth are correctly computed, completely supported and filed on time to avoid losses on the part of the enterprise for its non-collection.
observations.managementAction: Records for the previous years still have to be obtained due to the resignation of the assigned personnel. The management has identified the personnel to undergo the maintenance of the same.
observations.reason: No subsidiary ledger details were provided for Philhealth claims for CY 2018 and below. Collections recorded in CY 2021 was only ₱92,680.00. Amounting to ₱10,496,216.62 for 2018 and below.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Administrator to review the duties and responsibilities of the personnel assigned in the Internal Control Division and assign only such functions as set forth in the Internal Audit Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management has identified personnel to man the Internal Audit Division. The Internal Audit Division still performs functions such as pre audit of vouchers.
observations.reason: Internal Audit Division still performs activities such as pre-audit of vouchers instead of full internal audit functions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the:
a. OIC City Accountant to exert all efforts to demand the immediate refund or return of the unrecouped advances or to deduct the same from any claims due to the concerned contractors from the City Government; and
b. City Legal Officer to file the corresponding legal action in court for damages as a consequence of the breach of contract made against the City Government.
observations.managementAction:
The City Legal Office sent demand letter dated October 28, 2021 to Schindler for the refund of the unrecouped advances, however, Schindler has offered offsetting as the city government has also payables to Schindler. Further meetings/negotiations will still be scheduled.
Negotiation on-going. Legal action is suspended pending the result of negotiation between the management and Schindler.
observations.reason: No refund or return of the unrecouped advances was monitored during the year. Negotiation ongoing. Legal action suspended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the OIC City Treasurer to collect the unpaid regulatory fees plus surcharges from Smart Globe Communications, Inc. and Digitel Mobile Philippines, Inc., including other civil cases that are within the scope and jurisdiction of the city ordinance.
observations.managementAction: Management will adhere to the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: As per verification, no collection was posted in CY 2021. Cases of Smart and telecommunications are at the RTC under case numbers 17150-16 and 17518, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the LCPMCC Market Master to intensify the advertisement of available commercial stalls at the Laoag City Commercial Complex. Likewise, we recommended that Management reassess the monthly rental fee taking into consideration the location of stalls, and consider improvement or maintenance of the same to attract more stallholders and generate additional income for the economic enterprise.
observations.managementAction: Operations at the LCPMCC was affected by the pandemic. Measures have been implemented like giving of discounts and no imposition of surcharges to entice/help stall owners to cope up with their losses.
observations.reason: More stalls were vacated which is attributed to the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. require the Inventory Committee to conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE in order to establish their physical existence and conditions and prepare a report thereon signed by the Inventory Committee and City Mayor. In addition, an Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Properties be accomplished to account for city properties that are already beyond economical repair to facilitate dropping of these from the books in case they are determined to be of no economic value in order to ensure that the balances of PPE accounts are fairly presented in the financial statements.
b. require the CGSO to determine the end-users accountable for the unaccounted PPE items, require them to produce the same, otherwise demand the return of the money value of the missing items if lost due to their fault.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee together with the representative of the City Mayor’s Office conducted a physical inventory of all the assets of the City Government. However, the Inventory and IIRUP is yet to be prepared along with other schedules for the one-time cleansing as provided in COA Circular 2020-006.
Compliance with the recommendation is still on-going. An inventory report of the physical count conducted by the Inventory Committee was already submitted to the Audit Team.
The CGSO is finalizing the schedules pertaining to unaccounted PPE items.
observations.reason: Inventory and IIRUP yet to be prepared; compliance still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management assign the Chief of Hospital to coordinate with the Local Finance Committee and to review the existing policies regarding the operations of LCGH and prepare the necessary report containing a team to evaluate the actual operation of the LCGH and make a comprehensive 5-year business plan. This includes formulating strategic measures to intensify collection of receivables, imposing additional cost-cutting measures and exercising prudence in the disbursement of expenses to avoid further incurrence of cash overdraft.
observations.managementAction: Management is trying to deal with the operation of the LCGH and find solution to pay for the accruing expenses. Management will form a team to evaluate the operation of the entity. The operation of the LCGH still depend largely on subsidies from the general fund for the year, which was further aggravated by the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: LCGH operations still depend largely on subsidies from the general fund; impacted by COVID-19 pandemic. This is reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Local Finance Committee to strictly observe the provisions of Sections 337 of RA No. 7160 where close monitoring of the utilization of funds be made by adopting a plan in the allocation of available funds, formulating strategic measures to intensify collection of receivables, imposing additional cost-cutting measures and exercising prudence in the disbursement of expenses to avoid further incurrence of cash overdraft.
observations.managementAction:
The operation of the entity was greatly affected by the pandemic. Management has endeavored to take necessary steps to formulate better strategies and agreements.
Management assured that they will adhere with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: LCGH is still behind in the payment of benefits to its employees as well as its creditors, especially consignment creditors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to coordinate with the City Budget Officer to cause the preparation of the reconciliation statement of their respective records and to furnish the Audit Team a copy thereof for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting and Budget Office has partially started the Year End reconciliation. The late submission of trial balances and financial statements was partly caused by the delay in the reconciliation between the records of the Accounting and Budget Office.
observations.reason: Reconciliation not completed; late submission of trial balances and financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the CPPDO to:
c. expedite the implementation of all the identified development projects in order not to delay the services and benefits that could have been derived from its immediate implementation.
observations.managementAction:
Management assured the Audit Team that they will adhere with the audit recommendations. The partial implementation was because of restrictions due to the pandemic.
The project status of the sanitary landfill is at 80% as of December 31, 2021. However, other developmental projects were still not implemented.
observations.reason: Partial implementation due to COVID-19 pandemic; some projects still not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Point to ensure that the GAD AR is based on and consistent with the endorsed GPB, and to monitor the PPAs implementation of the PPAs responsive to GAD.
observations.managementAction: Management gave an assurance to adhere with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: For CY 2021, the GAD AR was based on the endorsed GPB. The implementation of some however, was not fully attained because of restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with Heads of implementing offices to send demand letters to qualified recipients for long due accounts and exert utmost effort for their immediate settlement in order to have more available funds to extend to other qualified beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office notified the departments that are in-charge in extending receivables to intensify collections.
observations.reason: Balances of Other Receivables from animal dispersal and livelihood assistance from 2011-2014 were ₱991,365.00 and ₱291,500.00, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to observe the IPSAS in the recording of transactions, install accounting control in receivable accounts and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office wrote the Head of the Market and requested reconciliation. The Market and accounting records for stall rentals are still unreconciled.
observations.reason: Market and accounting records for stall rentals remain unreconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the City Assessor to give preferential attention to the titling of the lots that are not yet in the name of the City Government pursuant to PD No. 1445 Section 39(2) and provide complete information on the four parcels of land for their proper identification.
We likewise recommended that Management require the City Accountant to reclassify the survey cost and taxes directly connected to the acquisition of lots as part of their costs.
observations.managementAction:
The Assessor’s Office has partially started the process of the titling of some lots owned by the city government. The City Accountant was not able to reclassify the survey cost and taxes directly connected in the acquisition of land.
The OIC-City Assessor has started gathering data for the several land accounts.
Verification of ledgers showed that 4 items were still not reclassified, namely: (1) 201-021-Land 875109-021-002; (2) 201-021-Land875110-021-002; (3) 201-021-Land875110-021-004; and (4) 201-021-Land 875109-021-003. The other 5 items were reclassified under JEV Nos. 2018-04-002753 and 2018-04-002760.
observations.reason: Partial actions: some titling processes started; reclassification of survey costs still pending for certain items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with the City Budget Officer and City Treasurer to strictly observe the provisions of Sections 305, 337 and 344 of RA No. 7160, adopt a plan in the allocation of available funds, intensify collection of receivables and exercise prudence in the disbursement of expenses to avoid further incurrence of cash overdraft.
observations.managementAction: Management continued to exercise prudence in managing the fiscal affairs of the city as well as to continue to intensify collections not only on receivables but also on taxes and revenues, and to adopt cost cutting measures that can generate savings from appropriations so that the City Government could be able to deliver the necessary services to the public and be able to fully fund the payments of its current obligations as well as fund future operations.
observations.reason: Ongoing efforts; City still working to comply, particularly regarding LCGH operations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to determine possible causes of delayed/non-submission of vouchers and act on it in order to comply with the prompt submission of monthly receipts and disbursement records in compliance to Section 7.2.1 of the Rules and Procedures on the Settlement of Accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office continuously coordinates with the Office of the City Treasurer for the prompt submission of vouchers to COA.
observations.reason: Submission of vouchers still not within 10 days of the ensuing month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to require the submission of the liquidation report on the utilization of the ₱93,780.00 and supporting documents on the procurement of 200 monobloc chairs and table; liquidation report and supporting documents for the amount of ₱45,000.00 released to William R. Peralta on October 14, 2015, President, Federation of Senior Citizens Association of Pasuquin, Ilocos Norte (FSCAP) for the purchase of monobloc chairs; and the amount of ₱20,010.00 released to Rex Epifanio Camaquin on November 10, 2015, Barangay Chairman of Barangay No. 32 Cabangaran, Vintar, Ilocos Norte for the purchase of diesel crude oil for the improvement of barangay roads.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant requested the assistance of the Office of the City Mayor for the compliance of the AOM 2018-003 (2017) dated April 2, 2018. No liquidation reports and supporting documents has been submitted to COA for payments.
observations.reason: No liquidation reports and supporting documents submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Budget Officer to source the operating expenses of the Product Display Center from the income generated from its operations and not from the coffers of the City Government.
observations.managementAction: The income from operations of the Pasalubong Center further declined in CY 2019 due to the road clearing directive of DILG and the COVID-19 pandemic. Management did not reflect the wages of the personnel aside from other operating expenses which would have resulted to a net loss.
observations.reason: Result of operations of Pasalubong Center not enough to cover wages and operating expenses; reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:
a. BAC Secretariat to submit the enumerated bidding documents in compliance to RA No. 9184.
f. City Accountant to submit the pertinent documents to account for the ₱415,400.00 not reflected in the GL for Due to Other NGAs.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC submitted the documentary requirements for the supplemental feeding to COA. However, the documents were evaluated to be insufficient. No further submission was made.
Management assured the submission of the pertinent documents. The BAC submitted documents but were insufficient to prove compliance to the COA Circular.
observations.reason: Documents submitted were insufficient; no further submission was made. No refund/return of unaccounted amount monitored.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to instruct all Heads of Departments to exercise austerity measure and comply with the provisions of the travel law and to refund excessive and unnecessary disbursements made.
observations.managementAction: Management requested for the consideration of the computations of the expenses incurred as these were duly certified from the head of the agency that these were absolutely necessary and were supported with actual bills and receipts. Nevertheless, the management would take into consideration the recommendation of the COA in the conduct of ISO implementation.
observations.reason: Excessive amount charged for ISO not yet returned by the concerned personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to comply with the guidelines and documentary requirements on the use of intelligence funds as provided under COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-001.
observations.managementAction: Management assured its compliance with the guidelines and documentary requirements on the use of intelligence funds as provided under COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-001. No Liquidation Report was submitted to the ICFAU for payments of subsistence and transportation of informers. This is reiterated in Part II of this report.
observations.reason: No Liquidation Report submitted to ICFAU; reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with the City Treasurer for the return to the source agencies of the unutilized balances of completed PPAs amounting to ₱891,184.03 in compliance to COA Circular No. 94-03.
observations.managementAction: Management assured the Audit Team they will comply. Amounts ascertained as balances will be remitted by the City Treasurer’s Office to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason: There were still remaining balances that were not returned to the source agency. Reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to require the recipients of the funds to submit liquidation reports with supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Management sent letters to the recipients. However, the documents were not yet submitted.
observations.reason: No liquidation reports submitted by recipients.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to require the Heads of implementing departments/units to implement the programmed projects so that the purpose for which these funds were granted is achieved.
observations.managementAction: Management did not implement the programmed projects. There was no implementation of programmed projects from the PDAF of Sen. Pia Cayetano amounting to ₱380,690.00 and financial assistance from Congressman Emmeline Aglipay amounting to ₱100,000.00.
observations.reason: No implementation of projects; reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the NGOs/POs and recipients to submit proper liquidation reports and return unutilized funds where applicable; that the City Agriculture Office follow-up additional liquidation papers; that the Heads of implementing offices expedite implementation of PDAF-funded projects so as not to defeat the purpose for which the funds were given.
observations.managementAction: The 80 Barangay Councils for Women submitted liquidation papers but were evaluated to be insufficient. An ND was issued for one Barangay Council for Women amounting to ₱10,000.00. Additional documents were submitted to support the liquidation but were insufficient to prove compliance. Some NGOs/POs such as FACES, Delta Sigma IX Confraternity of Laoag City, PNP Retirees Association of Laoag City & Ilocos Norte and BJMP Laoag City submitted liquidation reports except for some. The City Agriculture Office required the recipients to submit proper liquidation reports; four recipients submitted justifications; the ten recipients submitted liquidation reports but several were incomplete and need further verification; two recipients provided proofs of establishing vermin beds. For PDAF projects, management assured compliance and reported ₱1,919,133.44 remained unutilized as of December 31, 2021.
observations.reason: Documents submitted were insufficient; refunds not made where required; several liquidation reports incomplete; PDAF-funded projects partially unimplemented with remaining unutilized balances.

observations.recommendation:
1.9. We recommended that Management direct:
a. the OIC-City Treasurer to furnish the OIC-City Accountant a certified
list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible as of January 1
of the year then it shall be used as basis in setting up the RPT and SET
Receivable at the beginning of the same year;
b. the OIC-City Treasurer to intensify and maximize the collection of RPT
and SET by sending Notice of Delinquency to concerned taxpayers; and
c. the OIC-City Accountant to prepare adjusting entries for the
understatement of collectibles:
General Fund:
RPT Receivable 775,289.08
Deferred RPT 775,289.08
Special Education Fund:
SET Receivable 387,644.54
Deferred SET 387,644.54

2.6. We recommended that Management implement the following:
a. Make the necessary adjustment in the books to recognize the receivable
account and adjust the expense recognized through the following journal
entry:
Other Receivables 488,039.21
Due to Officers and Employees 329,658.40
Prior Year’s Adjustment 817,697.61
b. Facilitate the liquidation of the amount by submitting the documents
required under the JC to the ICFAU, or the collection of the amount from
the concerned accountable officer who disbursed the funds.
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct to the audit recommendation.
observations.managementAction:
Management adhered to the audit recommendation. Implemented.
The adjusting journal entry was recorded under Journal Entry Voucher Nos. GF-2021-03-002562, 2563 SEF- 2021-03-000204 TF-2021-03-000098 dated March 31, 2021 to effect the adjustment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
a. CGSO to facilitate the transfer of ownership of the Buildings and Other Structures to the barangays; 
b. OIC City Accountant to derecognize the PPE under Buildings and Other Structures accounts that are not proper assets of the City Government with the following adjusting journal entries:
Prior Years’ Adjustment 22,359,968.53
Buildings 5,486,093.56
Other Structures 16,873,874.97
c. OIC City Accountant to direct the barangay bookkeepers to record the fair value as at date of acquisition of the transferred PPE in the books of the respective barangays and thereafter furnish a copy of the JEV to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction:
CGSO prepared the Property Transfer Receipt.
The OIC City Accountant derecognized the PPE accounts in the books of the LGU.
The barangay bookkeepers were instructed to record in the books of the barangays the receipt of property.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
a. CGSO to conduct an inventory of the local roads under the City Government’s jurisdiction, prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) for Calendar Year 2021 and every year thereafter, and maintain a Road Map;
b. City Engineer to periodically provide the City Accountant and the CGSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and
c. City Assessor to immediately coordinate with the City Engineer for the assessment of the related road lots and provide the list of assessed values to the City Accountant and CGSO for proper recording thereof; and
d. City Accountant to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components, depreciate separately the road components, and immediately correct the erroneous depreciation and misclassified items recorded.
observations.managementAction:
CGSO already listed the component of each LRN in their Property Card.
Accounting Office is updating its Subsidiary Ledgers.
City Assessor listed the assessed values of the local road network based on the inventory of the City Engineering Office.
The City Accountant has complied with the recommendation and maintained subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the OIC City Accountant to prepare an aging assessment of the conditions and collectability of the Accounts Receivable and Other Receivables including an accurate aging thereof and based from its result, set-up allowance for impairment to present the net realizable value.
observations.managementAction: Accounting Office prepared an extensive aging for the receivables and served as the basis in determining the Allowance for Impairment Loss recognized at 2021 Year-end FS amounting to ₱1,348,679.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management request the SP to enact the necessary resolution to ratify the contracts entered into utilizing the BGCM fund to cure the defect in its implementation. Likewise, we recommended that Management furnish the Audit Team a copy of said resolution once the same has been approved.
observations.managementAction:
The matter was already taken up in the special session of the Sangguniang Panlungsod on March 31, 2021 with Resolution No, 2021-028 S. 2021.
A copy of the resolution was furnished to the City Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the OIC City Treasurer to pay allowances and honoraria such as PHIC shares of hospital employees and allowances of prosecutors by bank debit or by check; and the OIC City Accountant to ensure that the amount of cash advance granted to the Accountable Officers is within the limits of their maximum cash accountabilities.
We likewise recommended and Management agreed that if greater cash advance could not be avoided, instruct the OIC accountable officer to request an increase in the amount of the bond to an amount corresponding to the needed accountability.
observations.managementAction:
Accounting is closely monitoring cash advances to avoid such overlapping.
Payment of this kind were already made thru their ATM or by check.
The maximum accountability per accountable officer is considered before grant of cash advance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the OIC City Accountant to:
a. stop charging expenses that are not valid expenditures for the operation of public schools in accordance with Section 4 of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017; and
b. bill the City Division Office of Laoag the total charges to the fund in payment of their electricity consumption starting from CY 2013 up to the last electric bill paid by the City Government.
observations.managementAction:
The electricity consumption of the DEPED City Division Office of Laoag was already not billed and paid under the SEF.
A statement of account was already sent to the Division Office. The Accounting Office furnished the Audit Team with a copy of the SOA received by DepEd.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the CGSO to issue serially numbered Driver’s Trip Ticket and instruct the drivers to properly fill out the document after every travel to adequately support the charges to the Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expense account and also indicate the name of passengers in each trip ticket to support the necessity of the trip and maintain transparency in the use of government resources.
observations.managementAction: The Driver’s Trip Ticket is centrally issued by the CGSO and serially numbered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Human Resource Management Officer to evaluate the current roster of consultants and assess the need to engage their services and not to hire consultants whose functions are redundant to the functions of existing employees in the City Government and are not highly technical or specialized in nature.
observations.managementAction: The charging of honorarium from the consultancy budget was stopped upon the receipt of AOM. The consultants were rehired as contract of service until December 31, 2021 and no charges to the Consultancy services was monitored.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct all end-users to meticulously determine the project requirements and the corresponding budget estimate of their respective PPAs, giving due consideration to the conduct of market research and the different cost components thereof as provided under the LGU Procurement Manual.
observations.managementAction: ABC is already being filled out for the different cost components. Adherence to the audit recommendation is already observed in subsequent procurement projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BAC to post the invitations, notices of award and notices to proceed together with the approved contracts for the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects in the agency website within the prescribed period as required under Sections 21, 37 and 54 of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and IT department has already updated the contents of the agency website relating to the procurement projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BAC Chairman to invite only independent and knowledgeable members from NGOs, duly registered with the SEC or CDA, to serve as observers in the different stages of the procurement activities of the City Government as set forth in Sections 13.1 to 13.2 of the IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: BAC invited the representative of Philippine Institute of Civil Engineers (PICE) as observer in the procurement activities of the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the website administrator to immediately coordinate with heads of offices to provide the required reports for the maintenance and regular updating of the City Government’s transparency seal in its website, as provided in Section 94 of the General Provisions of RA No. 11465 and to post the required reports in the utilization of the BGCM as required under Section 4.3 of DBM LBC No. 125 in order for the City Government to uphold openness in access to government information by the public which may involve or benefit them.
observations.managementAction: Management has updated the website for the needed reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the OIC City Accountant to coordinate with the CGSO to identify all PPEs that were already turned-over to the Department of Education and derecognize these PPEs to comply with the requirements of IPSAS 1 and 17.
observations.managementAction:
PPE for transfer was identified and adjusted in the books.
The PPE items was derecognized in the books of accounts thru JEV No. 2021-12-0000688.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the development of the sanitary landfill as mandated under RA No. 9003 to ensure the protection of the public from health hazards and prevent environmental degradation.
observations.managementAction:
The construction of the Sanitary Landfill is at 80% complete as of December 31, 2021.
Steps were carried out for development of sanitary landfill but management encountered a few setbacks in its implementation.
However, as per accomplishment report as at year end, the project is nearing its completion at 72.4%.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the CDRRMO to prepare annually a LDRRMFIP to support the preparation of the AIP incorporating the DRRM program in strict compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012, particularly Section 5.1.2 thereof, identifying in detail the PPAs to be charged against the LDRRMF, in order to ensure proper implementation as well as expense classification.
observations.managementAction:
Management adhered to the audit recommendation.
LDRRMFIP for CY 2021 has already been prepared in accordance with the circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to establish the validity of the bank reconciling items in order to arrive at the correct balance of the cash in bank accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Made the adjustment under JEV No. 2021-09-008720.
The reconciling item was adjusted as a deduction from the book balance and recorded as additional accountability of the former City Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the CDRRMO to prepare a LDRRMFIP with the details of the funded projects and activities for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness pursuant to Sections 5.1.1 and 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction:
Management adhered to the audit recommendation.
LDRRMFIP for CY 2021 has already been prepared in accordance with the circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require all end-users to furnish the Auditor copy of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within five days after perfection in adherence to COA Circular 2009-001 so that any deficiency noted in the review thereof could be immediately complied/rectified.
observations.managementAction: The management is exerting effort to comply the 5-day rule in submitting copies of contracts, purchase orders to COA. Contracts are submitted within the said period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to require the City Budget Officer to comply with the budgetary requirement of one percent (1%) of IRA for the Local Council for Protection of Children and spend the budget for purposes provided for in DILG Memorandum 2012-120.
observations.managementAction:
Management already directed concerned offices and agencies to comply with the recommendations.
For CY 2021, the City Government allocated ₱6,259,200.00 for PPAs under LCPC fund which is compliant to the minimum budgetary requirement of 1% of IRA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that PPE for transfer will be
observations.managementAction: Management instruct the OIC City Accountant to coordinate with the CGSO to identify all PPEs that were already turned-over to the Department of Education and derecognize these PPEs to comply with the requirements of IPSAS 1 and 17. The recommendation will be implemented upon normalization of conditions brought about by COVID 19 pandemic.
observations.reason: The PPE items are still included in the ledger balances of the City Government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the OIC City Accountant to coordinate with the CGSO to prepare the required reports on Road Networks in accordance with the accounting and reporting guidelines, especially in the recognition of the various components of the Road Networks, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: Management held a meeting with the offices concerned.
observations.reason: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendations due to restricted movements brought about by the pandemic. This is reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the OIC – City Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for the account Due from NGAs-PhilHealth Claims and regularly update the same and ensure that all claims from PhilHealth are correctly computed, completely supported and filed on time to avoid losses on the part of the enterprise for its non-collection.
observations.managementAction: Maintained subsidiary ledgers for Philhealth claims for 2019 and 2020 amounting to ₱3,704,755.88 and ₱8,742,394.84, respectively.
observations.reason: No subsidiary ledger details were provided for Philhealth claims for CY 2018 and below amounting to ₱10,588,896.62.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the OIC City Accountant to assess the conditions of the Accounts Receivable and Other Receivables, prepare an accurate aging and set-up allowance for impairment, if necessary, to reflect the actual net realizable value.
observations.managementAction: Impairment will be effected and reflected for the 2021 financial statements. Management has minimized procured Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies through consignment for 2020, as such, the recording of the supplies was already in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009. Management committed that they will send demand letters, exert effort to determine the whereabouts of the debtors and correspondingly recognize the impairment loss as may be deemed appropriate.
observations.reason: Management has been sending demand letters to the recipients; however close monitoring could not be implemented due to the number of recipients. This is reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 4 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that PPE for transfer will be Not Implemented. Management instruct the OIC identified in coordination with the CGSO to identify all PPEs that were already turned-over to the Department of Education and derecognize these PPEs to comply with the requirements of IPSAS 1 and 17.
observations.managementAction: Management has not implemented the recommendation. The PPE items are still included in the ledger balances of the City Government.
observations.reason: The PPE items are still included in the ledger balances of the City Government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the OIC City Accountant to coordinate with the CGSO to prepare the required reports on Road Networks in accordance with the accounting and reporting guidelines, especially in the recognition of the various components of the Road Networks, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: Management held a meeting with the offices concerned. Management was not able to implement the audit recommendations due to restricted movements brought about by the pandemic.
observations.reason: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendations due to restricted movements brought about by the pandemic. This is reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the OIC – City Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for the account Due from NGAs-PhilHealth Claims and regularly update the same and ensure that all claims from PhilHealth are correctly computed, completely supported and filed on time to avoid losses on the part of the enterprise for its non-collection.
observations.managementAction: Maintained subsidiary ledgers for Philhealth claims for 2019 and 2020 amounting to ₱3,704,755.88 and ₱8,742,394.84, respectively.
observations.reason: No subsidiary ledger details were provided for Philhealth claims for CY 2018 and below amounting to ₱10,588,896.62.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the OIC City Accountant to assess the conditions of the Accounts Receivable and Other Receivables, prepare an accurate aging and set-up allowance for impairment, if necessary, to reflect the actual net realizable value.
observations.managementAction: Impairment will be effected and reflected for the 2021 financial statements. Management has been sending demand letters to the recipients; however close monitoring could not be implemented due to the number of recipients.
observations.reason: This is reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 4 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Administrator to review the duties and responsibilities of the personnel assigned in the Internal Control Division and assign only such functions as set forth in the Internal Audit Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management reorganized the department, however, verification revealed that they still perform functions such as pre-audit of vouchers.
observations.reason: Verification revealed that Internal Control Division still perform pre-audit of vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC Chairman and BAC Members to evaluate carefully all documents submitted by the bidders and strictly adhere to the IRR of RA No. 9184. The liable officers are required to refund the total amount of the contract in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-003.
observations.managementAction: Submitted an Appeal Memorandum to COA Regional Office I (COA RO I) for the Notice of Disallowance (ND).
observations.reason: COA RO I affirmed the ND issued by the Audit Team under COA RO I Decision No. 2021-007. Management has filed a petition for review of the disallowance before the COA Commission Proper.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the:
a. OIC City Accountant to exert all efforts to demand the immediate refund or return of the unrecouped advances or deduct the same from any claims due to the concerned contractors from the City Government; and
b. City Legal Officer to file the corresponding legal action in court for damages as a consequence of the breach of contract made against the City Government.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has been in constant communication with the Engineering Office for the status of the account. The LCGH still has transactions with Jardine Schindler Elevator Corp. The legal office staff is yet to look on the details of the said transaction.
observations.reason: The LCGH still has transactions with Jardine Schindler Elevator Corp. The legal office staff is yet to look into details and no legal action initiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the development of the sanitary landfill as mandated under RA No. 9003 to ensure the protection of the public from health hazards and prevent environmental degradation.
observations.managementAction: The City Government started paying the owner of lots for the sanitary landfill. Recently, the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the City Government was approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission and the DENR. The procurement project for the Establishment/Development of Sanitary Landfill was posted in the PhilGEPS on March 2021.
observations.reason: Steps were carried out for development of sanitary landfill but encountered a few setbacks in the acquisition of lots for the location. The procurement project posted in March 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the OIC - City Treasurer to collect the unpaid regulatory fees plus surcharges from Smart Communications, Inc. and Digitel Mobile Philippines, Inc., including other telecommunication companies that are within the scope and jurisdiction of the city ordinance.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-City Treasurer has been in coordination with the Telecommunication Companies to settle this issue.
observations.reason: As per verification, no collection was posted in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Market Master to intensify the advertisement of available commercial stalls at the Laoag City Commercial Complex. Likewise, we recommended that Management reassess the monthly rental fee taking into consideration the location of stalls, and consider improvement or maintenance of the same to attract more stallholders and generate additional income for the economic enterprise.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation. More stalls were vacated which is attributed to the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: There were still several stalls vacated due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the CDRRMO to prepare annually a LDRRMFIP to support the AIP incorporating the DRRM program in strict compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012, particularly Section 5.1.2 thereof, identifying in detail the PPAs to be charged against the LDRRMF, in order to ensure proper implementation as well as expense classification.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMFIP has been prepared for the next budget year.
observations.reason: LDRRMFIP for CY 2021 has already been prepared in accordance with the circular; the CY 2020 AIP was prepared without LDRRMFIP before the issuance of the audit observation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. require the Inventory Committee to conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE in order to establish their physical existence and conditions and prepare a report thereon signed by the Inventory Committee and City Mayor. In addition, an Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Properties be accomplished to account for city properties that are already beyond economical repair to facilitate dropping of these from the books in case they are determined to be of no economic value in order to ensure that the balances of PPE accounts are fairly presented in the financial statements.
b. require the CGSO to determine the end-users accountable for the unaccounted PPE items, require them to produce the same, otherwise demand the return of the money value of the missing items if lost due to their fault.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee together with the representative of the City Mayor’s Office conducted a physical inventory of all the assets of the City Government. Adjusting entries were already effected for some PPE accounts.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee conducted a physical inventory; the Inventory report is not yet submitted to the Audit Team for validation. Some PPE items still not reconciled; end-user/accountability not fully determined.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to consider non-payment of contracts entered into by the local chief executive for being irregular and illegal and facilitate return of the amount already paid amounting to ₱106,250.00.
observations.managementAction: Management did not process payments for the unauthorized contracts amounting to ₱106,250.00. Submitted an Appeal/Petition for Review to COA RO I and COA CP.
observations.reason: ND pertaining to incentives of barangay treasurers was already appealed; COA RO I Decision No. 2019-145 dated 11/22/19 affirmed disallowance; Management filed Petition for Review before COA CP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Budget Officer to partner with the City Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009, COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated, and Section 95 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The five NDs issued by the Audit Team in the total amount of ₱1,305,500.00 were appealed before the COA RO I and submitted Petition for Review before the COA CP.
observations.reason: NDs were appealed; one ND pertaining to incentives of barangay treasurers was issued and appealed; outcome pending with COA CP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the BAC Secretariat to follow strictly the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR and ensure that the Invitation to Bid, Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed are properly advertised in the required media and within the prescribed period to ensure wide dissemination of information and promote transparency and accountability in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The BAC was advised to fully comply with the recommendation of the COA.
observations.reason: Invitation, Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed were posted but not within the required time. This is reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 14 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BAC Chairman and BAC Members to strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR and be mindful of its duties and responsibilities thereunder, ensuring that all procurement be meticulously and judiciously planned to the smooth conduct of procurement activities, and that they are undertaken efficiently.
observations.managementAction: Submitted a Petition for Review before the COA CP for the Notice of Disallowance issued by the auditor.
observations.reason: Full compliance to the audit recommendation is not yet manifested in succeeding procurements; posting of NTP and NOA, furnishing of contract copies and supporting documents within required 5 days not consistently done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Head of the end-user department/unit to coordinate with the CGSO to submit a breakdown showing how the budget cost for the Rapid Response Vehicles was determined and an inventory report accounting the tools/equipment/accessories mounted on the vehicles to enhance accountability. We likewise recommended that Management instruct all end-users to prepare the ABC meticulously keeping in mind the cost considerations as prescribed in the Procurement Manual for LGUs for all succeeding procurements.
observations.managementAction: Submitted an Appeal to the COA RO I and Petition for Review before the COA CP.
observations.reason: Management failed to submit a breakdown of the budget cost and inventory report; ND 19-024-100 (2018) was issued and appealed.

observations.managementAction: Submitted an Appeal to the COA RO I to lift the ND issued. Whelen strobe lights were delivered but not yet installed. The lacking accessories were already delivered as per IAR that were furnished to the office. Management forfeited the retention fee of ₱91,000.00 and sent a demand letter for the balance of ₱1,106.30.
observations.reason: The ND was issued; retention fee carried as payable as of December 31, 2020; subsidiary ledger showed retention fee still carried as payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management assign the Chief of Hospital to coordinate with the Local Finance Committee to review the existing policies regarding the operations of LCGH and prepare the necessary report containing their recommendations especially on its profitability and submit it to the City Mayor for his guide and perusal.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor made staffing changes in the hospital and directed top management to take necessary steps for improvement of operations.
observations.reason: The LCGH operations still depend largely on subsidies from the general fund for the year, which was further aggravated by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Local Finance Committee to strictly observe the provisions of Sections 337 of RA No. 7160 where close monitoring of the utilization of funds be made by adopting a plan in the allocation of available funds, formulating strategic measures to intensify collection of receivables, imposing additional cost-cutting measures and exercising prudence in the disbursement of expenses to avoid further incurrence of cash overdraft.
observations.managementAction: The management evaluated the operation of the entity (LCGH) for the last 5 years in order to formulate better strategies.
observations.reason: LCGH is still behind in payment of benefits to its employees as well as to its creditors, especially on previous consignment agreements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 regarding timely liquidation of cash advances and to issue demand letters to officials/ employees with unliquidated cash advances otherwise, withhold their salaries as provided under Item 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Management has been strictly implementing the liquidation of cash advances. The liquidation of cash advances for travel was monitored and minimal for CY 2020.
observations.reason: There were still overlapping cash advances monitored; thus, reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 8 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to partner with the City Budget Officer to process payment of transactions that are not among those prohibited under COA Circular No. 2012-003.
observations.managementAction: Management appealed the disallowed payment before the COA RO I and Petition for Review was submitted to the COA CP.
observations.reason: COA RO I issued ND; Management filed Petition for Review before COA CP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to submit the authenticated copy of the Contract Agreement between the City Government and the lessor/owner and the SP Resolution authorizing the Local Chief Executive to enter into the Lease Contract.
observations.managementAction: Management produced a copy of the Contract Agreement only after an ND was issued by reason of its non-submission after the prescribed time set for compliance had expired. It was attached to the Appeal Memorandum. Management failed to produce an authenticated copy of the SP Resolution.
observations.reason: Contract Agreement produced only after ND; SP Resolution not produced. COA RO I affirmed the ND; Management filed Petition for Review before COA CP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to establish the validity of the bank reconciling items in order to arrive at the correct balance of the cash in bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office will record the adjusting entry in the subsequent year.
observations.reason: The amount was presented as a bank reconciling item (Deposit in Transit), however, the same should be a deduction from the book balance and recorded as additional accountability of the former City Treasurer. The deposit was verified to be non-existent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to coordinate with the City Budget Officer to cause the reconciliation. The Accounting and Budget Office should prepare reconciliation statements of their respective records and furnish copies for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The Budget Office has started the cause reconciliation. The late submission of Year End Trial Balances and financial statements was partly caused by the delay in the reconciliation.
observations.reason: Reconciliation initiated but not yet completed; delays affected year-end submissions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the CDRRMO to prepare a LDRRMFIP with the details of the funded projects and activities for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness pursuant to Sections 5.1.1 and 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMFIP has been prepared for the next budget year.
observations.reason: LDRRMFIP for CY 2021 prepared; the 2020 AIP was prepared without LDRRMFIP before the issuance of the audit observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the CPDO: a. to optimize the utilization of the 20% DF according to its intended purpose and undergo re-evaluation to determine what should be taken as a priority development project to be implemented; and b. expedite the implementation of all the identified development projects in order not to delay the services and benefits that could have been derived from its immediate implementation.
observations.managementAction: Management assured the Audit Team that they will adhere with the audit recommendations. Partial implementation was because of restrictions due to the pandemic.
observations.reason: Partial implementation; pandemic restrictions hindered full implementation. Reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 7.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management instruct the GAD Focal Point to ensure that the GAD AR is based on and consistent with the endorsed GPB, and to monitor the implementation of the PPAs responsive to GAD.
observations.managementAction: Management gave an assurance to adhere with the audit recommendation. For CY 2020, the GAD AR was based on the endorsed GPB. The implementation, however, was not fully attained because of restrictions due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Implementation was not fully attained because of COVID-19 related restrictions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management require the OIC - City Treasurer to furnish the City Accountant a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible at the beginning of each year as well as a list of real property tax delinquencies as of December 31, 2017 to record the actual receivables for real property taxes in the books of the agency.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is communicating with the CTO for the proper recording of RPT collection and receivable.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office did not set up the receivable accounts at the beginning of the year. Reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 5.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with Heads of implementing offices to send demand letters to qualified recipients for long due accounts and exert utmost effort for their immediate settlement in order to have more available funds to extend to other qualified beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office notified the departments that are in charge in extending receipts to recipients to intensify collections.
observations.reason: Balances of Other Receivables from animal dispersal and livelihood assistance from 2011-2014 remain uncollected (₱1,024,460 and ₱161,064.00 respectively).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to observe the IPSAS in the recording of transactions, install accounting control in receivable accounts and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office wrote to the Head of the Market to reconcile with the data as the Market former based its entries on the collection report being submitted by the Treasurer's Office.
observations.reason: Accounting and Market records for stall rentals remain unreconciled as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Assessor to give preferential attention to the titling of the lots that are not yet in the name of the City Government pursuant to PD No. 1445 Section 39(2) and provide the complete information on the four parcels of land for their proper identification. We likewise recommended that Management require the City Accountant to reclassify the survey cost and taxes directly connected to the acquisition of lots as part of their costs.
observations.managementAction: Management assured compliance with the audit recommendation. The OIC-City Assessor has started gathering data for several land accounts. Some items were reclassified under JEV Nos. 2018-04-002753 and 2018-04-002760. Four items remain not reclassified.
observations.reason: Verification showed 4 items still not reclassified; other 5 items reclassified. Titling and complete information still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with the City Budget Officer and City Treasurer to strictly observe the provisions of Sections 305, 337 and 344 of RA No. 7160, adopt a plan in the allocation of available funds, intensify collection of receivables and exercise prudence in the disbursement of expenses to avoid further incurrence of cash overdraft.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of complying with the audit recommendation, particularly regarding the operations of the LCGH. Management continues to intensify collections and adopt cost-cutting measures.
observations.reason: Partial steps taken; full compliance not yet achieved; LCGH operations still need improvements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to determine possible causes of delayed/non-submission of vouchers and act on it in order to comply with the prompt submission of monthly receipts and disbursement records in compliance to Section 7.2.1 of the Rules and Procedures on the Settlement of Accounts.
observations.managementAction: The management is exerting effort to comply; prompt submission of vouchers to COA is being pursued.
observations.reason: There were still several vouchers not submitted within the 10-day requirement of the ensuing month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to require the submission of the liquidation report on the utilization of the ₱93,780.00 and supporting documents on the procurement of 200 monobloc chairs and table; liquidation report and supporting documents for the amount of ₱45,000.00 released to William R. Peralta on October 14, 2015, President, Federation of Senior Citizens Association of Pasuquin, Ilocos Norte (FSCAP) for the purchase of monobloc chairs; and the amount of ₱20,010.00 released to Rex Epifanio Camaquin on November 10, 2015, Barangay Chairman of Barangay No. 32 Cabangaran, Vintar, Ilocos Norte for the purchase of diesel crude oil for the improvement of barangay roads.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant requested the assistance of the Office of the City Mayor for compliance of the AOM 2018-003 (2017).
observations.reason: No liquidation reports and supporting documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require all end-users to furnish the Auditor copy of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within five days after perfection in adherence to COA Circular 2009-001 so that any deficiency noted in the review thereof could be immediately complied/rectified.
observations.managementAction: The management is exerting effort to comply. There was a directive from the City Mayor for compliance to the AOM. Some contracts and documents were produced after the ND was issued.
observations.reason: Several contracts were not submitted within the required 5 days; compliance remains inconsistent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Budget Officer to comply with the budgetary requirement of one percent (1%) of IRA for the LCPC and spend the budget for purposes provided for in DILG Memorandum 2012-120.
observations.managementAction: Management directed concerned offices and agencies to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: For CY 2020, the City allocated only ₱200,000.00 for PPAs under LCPC fund, less than 1% of IRA requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Budget Officer to source the operating expenses of the Product Display Center from the income generated from its operations and not from the coffers of the City Government.
observations.managementAction: Its income further declined in CY 2019 due to road clearing directive of DILG and the COVID-19 pandemic in CY 2020. The City is exploring options to generate savings from appropriations.
observations.reason: Income from the Pasalubong Center insufficient to cover personnel wages; negative net income in CYs 2019 and 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC Secretariat to submit the enumerated bidding documents in compliance to RA No. 9184; City Treasurer to open a separate account for the SFP Fund as required in the Omnibus Guidelines and transfer the remaining amount allocation; and City Accountant to submit the pertinent documents to account for the ₱415,400.00 not reflected in the GL for Due to Other NGAs.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted documentary requirements for the supplemental feeding; the City Treasurer prepared a separate account though no subsequent fund receipt after March 31, 2017. Management assured submission of pertinent documents for the ₱415,400.00.
observations.reason: Documents submitted were insufficient to account for the ₱415,400.00; no further submissions; fund balance as of Dec 31, 2020 was only ₱400.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to instruct all Heads of Departments to exercise austerity measure and comply with the travel rules and to refund excessive and unnecessary disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Management requested for the consideration of the computations of the expenses incurred as these were certified from the head of the agency that these were absolutely necessary and supported with actual bills and receipts. Management would take into consideration the recommendation of COA in the conduct of ISO implementation.
observations.reason: Excessive amounts charged for ISO were not returned by the concerned personnel; management to consider recommendations for future implementations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to comply with the guidelines and documentary requirements on the use of intelligence funds as provided under COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-001.
observations.managementAction: Management assured compliance but no liquidation report was submitted to ICFAU for payments of subsistence and transportation of informers. Amounts recorded under Intelligence Expenses and Extraordinary Expenses remain unadjusted and receivable from the Mayor/Special Disbursing Officer.
observations.reason: No liquidation reports submitted; amounts recorded remain unadjusted and receivable from Mayor/Special Disbursing Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with the City Treasurer for the return of unexpended balances of completed PPAs amounting to ₱891,184.03 in compliance to COA Circular No. 94-03.
observations.managementAction: Management assured the Audit Team the return of unexpended balances of completed programs was being processed though some balances remain unreturned.
observations.reason: Some balances still not returned to source agencies as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management instruct the City Accountant to require the recipients of the funds to submit the necessary liquidation reports with supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Management sent letters to the recipients but liquidation reports were not yet submitted.
observations.reason: No liquidation reports submitted by recipients.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management require the Heads of implementing departments/units to implement the programmed projects so that the purpose for which the fund was given is achieved.
observations.managementAction: Management did not implement the programmed projects; no further action taken. There was no implementation of projects from PDAF of Sen. Pia Cayetano (₱380,690.00) and financial assistance from Congressman Emmeline Aglipay (₱100,000.00).
observations.reason: No action taken to implement the projects; amounts remained unutilized.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the City Accountant to have the 80 Barangay Councils for Women return the fund they have received in the amount of ₱10,000.00 each for failure to account such fund.
observations.managementAction: The 80 Barangay Councils for Women through the CSWDO submitted some liquidation documents; however, one ND was issued for one Barangay Council for Women amounting to ₱10,000.00.
observations.reason: Liquidation reports submitted were inadequate; one ND issued for one Barangay Council; other issues remain unresolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to require the recipients to return the amount they have received and to see to it that funds granted should be within the guidelines set under Item 4.3 of COA Circular 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.
observations.managementAction: Additional documents were submitted to support the liquidation but were insufficient.
observations.reason: Documents insufficient to prove compliance with COA Circular; refund not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to require the federations to account for the funds granted and return unutilized funds.
observations.managementAction: No report was submitted by the federations to account for the funds.
observations.reason: No liquidation or accounting reports submitted by federations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Agriculture Office to follow-up and require recipients to submit proper liquidation reports and refund unutilized amounts.
observations.managementAction: Some recipients submitted justification and liquidation reports; others submitted incomplete liquidation reports. Two recipients provided proof they established vermin beds and used the funds accordingly.
observations.reason: Liquidation reports incomplete for several recipients; refunds not made by those who did not comply.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Agriculture Office to require the recipients to explain why they were not the ones who implemented the project, have the 10 recipients submit a liquidation report, and for the 2 recipients to return the amount of ₱20,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The City Agriculture Office required the recipients to submit liquidation reports. Four recipients submitted justifications; ten had submitted liquidation reports which were incomplete and require further verification. Two recipients who were required to refund provided proofs of establishment of vermin beds.
observations.reason: Some recipients provided justifications and evidence; others still pending refunds or further verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management instruct the City Treasurer to coordinate with the City Accountant to have the persons liable/responsible return the ₱150,000.00 and ensure that City Government’s funds are spent solely for public purpose as this is one of the fundamental principles in local fiscal administration under R.A. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management sent letters to the recipients for refund but the amount was not yet returned.
observations.reason: No refund made as of the latest validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management require the Heads of implementing offices/units to implement the projects pertaining to the ₱8,718,022.00 so as not to defeat the purpose for which the fund was given.
observations.managementAction: Management assured to implement and complied with some of the programmed PPAs, but portions remained unutilized as of Dec 31, 2020 (₱1,919,133.44 unutilized).
observations.reason: Partial utilization; ₱1,919,133.44 remained unutilized as of Dec 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the OIC City
Accountant to immediately reclassify the accounts Cash – Local Treasury, Cash in
Bank – LCSA and Cash in Bank – LCTD amounting to ₱1,772,111.39, ₱63,490,806.97
and ₱21,896,877.94, respectively, to the account Other Receivables for fair
presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
a. CGSO to facilitate the transfer of ownership of the Buildings and Other
Structures to the barangays;
b. OIC City Accountant to derecognize the PPE under Buildings and Other
Structures accounts that are not proper assets of the City Government with the
following adjusting journal entries:

Prior Years’ Adjustment 22,359,968.53
Buildings 5,486,093.56
Other Structures 16,873,874.97

c. OIC City Accountant to direct the barangay bookkeepers to record the fair value
as at date of acquisition of the transferred PPE in the books of the respective
barangays and thereafter furnish a copy of the JEV to the Audit Team

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
a. CGSO to conduct an inventory of the local roads under the City Government’s
jurisdiction, prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network
(RPCLRN) for Calendar Year 2021 and every year thereafter, and maintain a
Road Map;
b. City Engineer to periodically provide the City Accountant and the CGSO with the
complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
c. City Assessor to immediately coordinate with the City Engineer for the assessment
of the related road lots and provide the list of assessed values to the City Accountant
and CGSO for proper recording thereof; and
d. City Accountant to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road and its
components, depreciate separately the road components, and immediately correct
the erroneous depreciation and misclassified items recorded.

We recommended that Management direct the OIC City Accountant to
prepare an assessment of the conditions and collectability of the Accounts Receivable
and Other Receivables including an accurate aging thereof and based from its result,
set-up allowance for impairment to present the net realizable value.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
a. OIC - City Treasury Office to furnish the OIC – City Accountant a certified list
of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible at the beginning of the year as
well as the updated list of Real Property Tax delinquencies as of December 31,
2020; and
b. OIC – Accounting Office to record in the books the actual receivables for the
delinquent Real Property Units provided by the City Treasury Office and prepare
adjusting entries for the errors committed in the recording of the advanced
payment:

General Fund:
For the tax delinquencies:
RPT Receivable 30,398,941.70
Deferred RPT 30,398,941.70
For the misclassification:
Other Deferred Credits 28,406,515.20
Deferred RPT 28,406,515.20
Special Education Fund:
For the tax delinquencies:
SET Receivable 15,199,469.17
Deferred SET 15,199,469.17
For the double recording of advanced payment:
Other Deferred Credits 13,923,819.96
SET Receivable 13,923,819.96

We recommended that Management request the SP to enact the necessary
resolution to ratify the contracts entered into utilizing the BGCM fund to cure the
defect in its implementation. Likewise, we recommended that Management furnish
the Audit Team a copy of said resolution once the same has been approved.

We recommended that Management instruct the:
a. Head of the City Planning and Development Council to closely coordinate with its
members to conduct regular monitoring and undergo re-evaluation of PPAs to
determine what among the priority development projects are to be implemented in
order to optimally utilize the 20% DF according to its intended purpose; and
b. Implementing offices’ Department Heads to follow the timelines and schedules as
indicated in the Annual Procurement Plan in the implementation of the identified
development projects in order not to delay the services and benefits that could be
derived from their immediate implementation.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the OIC City
Treasurer to pay allowances and honoraria such as PHIC shares of hospital
employees and allowances of prosecutors by bank debit or by check; and the OIC
City Accountant to ensure that the amount of cash advance granted to the
Accountable Officers is within the limits of their maximum cash accountabilities.

We likewise recommended and Management agreed that if greater cash
advance could not be avoided, instruct the OIC City Treasurer to request an increase
in the amount of the bond to an amount corresponding to the needed accountability.

We recommended that Management instruct the OIC City Accountant to:
a. stop charging expenses that are not valid expenditures for the operation of public
schools in accordance with Section 4 of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1
s. 2017; and
b. bill the City Division Office of Laoag the total charges to the fund in payment of
their electricity consumption starting from CY 2013 up to the last electric bill paid
by the City Government.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the CGSO to issue
serially numbered Driver’s Trip Ticket and instruct the drivers to properly fill out
the document after every travel to adequately support the charges to the Fuel, Oil and
Lubricants Expense account and also indicate the name of passengers in each trip
ticket to support the necessity of the trip and maintain transparency in the use of
government resources.

We recommended that Management instruct the City Human Resource
Management Officer to evaluate the current roster of consultants and assess the need
to engage their services and not to hire consultants whose functions are redundant to
the functions of existing employees in the City Government and are not highly
technical or specialized in nature.

We recommended that Management instruct all end-users to meticulously
determine the project requirements and the corresponding budget estimate of their
respective PPAs, giving due consideration to the conduct of market research and the
different cost components thereof as provided under the LGU Procurement Manual.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the City Engineer to integrate the CSHP in the computation of
construction costs of all infrastructure projects to be implemented by the City
Government to ensure the protection and welfare of the workers at the
construction site as well as the general public passing around the construction
sites; and
b. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairperson to require the
contractors to submit DOLE-approved CSHP to ensure the latter’s compliance
with Occupational Safety and Health Standards set by the DOLE.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat to post the
invitations, notices of award and notices to proceed together with the approved
contracts for the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects in the
agency website within the prescribed period as required under Sections 21, 37 and 54
of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC Chairman to invite only
independent and knowledgeable members from NGOs, duly registered with the SEC
or CDA, to serve as observers in the different stages of the procurement activities of
the City Government as set forth in Sections 13.1 to 13.2 of the IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the website
administrator to immediately coordinate with heads of offices to provide the required
reports for the maintenance and regular updating of the City Government’s
transparency seal in its website, as provided in Section 94 of the General Provisions
of RA No. 11465 and to post the required reports in the utilization of the BGCM as
required under Section 4.3 of DBM LBC No. 125 in order for the City Government
to uphold openness in access to government information by the public which may
involve or benefit them.
